\clearpage

# Figures

<!--
Auto-generated by the BET results task. Edit this file in the report repository to choose, remove, reorder, or rewrite figures.
If this section file is deleted, the next report job will seed a fresh copy from generated/outputs/report-ready/figures.qmd.
-->

## Main figure set

### CPUE observed and fitted

<!-- figure: cpue-fits -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Observed and fitted CPUE. Points show observed index values and fitted lines show model predictions; facets and legends identify selected fisheries, CPUE series, and assessment models.](generated/outputs/figures/cpue-fits.png){#fig-cpue-fits fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### CPUE regional share

<!-- figure: cpue-regional-share -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Regional CPUE share compared with regional biomass share. Bars compare the regional distribution of the selected CPUE series and CPUE scaling target with the selected biomass basis, highlighting scale or spatial consistency across regions.](generated/outputs/figures/cpue-regional-share.png){#fig-cpue-regional-share fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Total catch observed and fitted

<!-- figure: total-catch-fits -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Total catch observed and fitted. The figure uses the labels, colours, facets, and legends shown in the plot to identify scenarios, fisheries, regions, years, or diagnostics selected in mfclshiny. It is generated from the Catch view.](generated/outputs/figures/total-catch-fits.png){#fig-total-catch-fits fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Catch observed and fitted by fishery

<!-- figure: catch-by-fishery-fits -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Catch observed and fitted by fishery. The figure uses the labels, colours, facets, and legends shown in the plot to identify scenarios, fisheries, regions, years, or diagnostics selected in mfclshiny. It is generated from the Catch view.](generated/outputs/figures/catch-by-fishery-fits.png){#fig-catch-by-fishery-fits fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Key quantities

<!-- figure: key-quantities -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Key derived quantities. Panels show dynamic spawning depletion \(SB/SB\[F=0\]\), recruitment, spawning potential, and annual instantaneous fishing mortality through time. Lines identify the selected assessment models; colours or line styles distinguish models when multiple scenarios are plotted. The depletion panel includes dashed reference lines at 0.2 and 0.5.](generated/outputs/figures/key-quantities.png){#fig-key-quantities fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Depletion by area

<!-- figure: depletion-by-area -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Dynamic spawning depletion by model region. Panels or line groups identify model areas, and colours identify selected assessment models so regional depletion patterns can be compared through time.](generated/outputs/figures/depletion-by-area.png){#fig-depletion-by-area fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Recruitment by area

<!-- figure: recruitment-by-area -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Estimated annual recruitment by model region. Panels or line groups identify model areas, and colours identify selected assessment models so spatial recruitment patterns can be compared through time.](generated/outputs/figures/recruitment-by-area.png){#fig-recruitment-by-area fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Kobe plot

<!-- figure: kobe-plot -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Kobe stock-status trajectory. Points and connecting paths show the trajectory through time relative to biomass and fishing-intensity reference axes; colours identify selected assessment models.](generated/outputs/figures/kobe-plot.png){#fig-kobe-plot fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Majuro plot

<!-- figure: majuro-plot -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Majuro stock-status trajectory. Points and connecting paths show depletion and fishing intensity through time; colours identify selected assessment models and reference zones aid stock-status interpretation.](generated/outputs/figures/majuro-plot.png){#fig-majuro-plot fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Juvenile and adult fishing mortality by area

<!-- figure: f-juvenile-adult-by-area -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Juvenile and adult annual instantaneous fishing mortality by model region. Panels or line groups separate juvenile and adult components and identify regional differences through time.](generated/outputs/figures/f-juvenile-adult-by-area.png){#fig-f-juvenile-adult-by-area fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Area contribution to fishing mortality

<!-- figure: f-area-contribution -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Regional contribution to total annual instantaneous fishing mortality. Stacked or faceted values show how each model area contributes to total F through time for the selected assessment models.](generated/outputs/figures/f-area-contribution.png){#fig-f-area-contribution fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Spawning potential with and without fishing

<!-- figure: spawning-potential-with-without-fishing -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Spawning potential with and without fishing. Lines compare estimated fishing conditions with no-fishing reference conditions through time for the selected assessment models.](generated/outputs/figures/spawning-potential-with-without-fishing.png){#fig-spawning-potential-with-without-fishing fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Total biomass with and without fishing

<!-- figure: total-biomass-with-without-fishing -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Total biomass with and without fishing. Lines compare estimated fishing conditions with no-fishing reference conditions through time for the selected assessment models.](generated/outputs/figures/total-biomass-with-without-fishing.png){#fig-total-biomass-with-without-fishing fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Region map

<!-- figure: region-map -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Region map. The figure uses the labels, colours, facets, and legends shown in the plot to identify scenarios, fisheries, regions, years, or diagnostics selected in mfclshiny. It is generated from the Region Map view.](generated/outputs/figures/region-map.png){#fig-region-map fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Tag reporting rates

<!-- figure: tagging-dynamics -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Estimated tag reporting rates. Distributions or intervals summarize reporting-rate estimates by tag reporting group; colours and facets identify selected assessment models or grouping variables.](generated/outputs/figures/tagging-dynamics.png){#fig-tagging-dynamics fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Tag returns over time

<!-- figure: tag-returns-all -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Observed tag returns over time, with all recapture fisheries combined. Lines and facets identify selected assessment models and the temporal pattern of total tag returns.](generated/outputs/figures/tag-returns-all.png){#fig-tag-returns-all fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Tag returns by recapture group

<!-- figure: tag-returns-by-group -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Observed tag returns over time by recapture group. Panels or colours identify recapture groups and selected assessment models.](generated/outputs/figures/tag-returns-by-group.png){#fig-tag-returns-by-group fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Tag attrition

<!-- figure: tag-attrition-all -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Tag attrition through time, with all recapture fisheries combined. Lines summarize the decline in available tag cohorts through time for selected assessment models.](generated/outputs/figures/tag-attrition-all.png){#fig-tag-attrition-all fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Tag attrition by program

<!-- figure: tag-attrition-by-program -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Tag attrition through time by tagging program. Panels or colours identify tag programs and selected assessment models.](generated/outputs/figures/tag-attrition-by-program.png){#fig-tag-attrition-by-program fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Tag attrition by recapture region

<!-- figure: tag-attrition-by-region -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Tag attrition through time by recapture region. Panels or colours identify regions and selected assessment models.](generated/outputs/figures/tag-attrition-by-region.png){#fig-tag-attrition-by-region fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Regional movement

<!-- figure: regional-movement -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Estimated quarterly regional movement. Matrix cells show movement probabilities from origin to destination regions; facets identify quarters or assessment models where available.](generated/outputs/figures/regional-movement.png){#fig-regional-movement fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Stock-recruitment relationship

<!-- figure: population-biology -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Stock-recruitment relationship. Points or lines show annual recruitment against adult biomass or spawning biomass; facets and legends identify selected assessment models.](generated/outputs/figures/population-biology.png){#fig-population-biology fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Maturity at age

<!-- figure: maturity-at-age -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Maturity at age. Curves show the proportion mature by age class for selected assessment models.](generated/outputs/figures/maturity-at-age.png){#fig-maturity-at-age fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Natural mortality at age

<!-- figure: natural-mortality-at-age -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Natural mortality at age. Curves show the assumed or estimated natural mortality schedule by age class for selected assessment models.](generated/outputs/figures/natural-mortality-at-age.png){#fig-natural-mortality-at-age fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Growth curve

<!-- figure: growth-curve -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Growth curve. Lines show predicted mean length at age, and any shaded band indicates the configured growth uncertainty interval for selected assessment models.](generated/outputs/figures/growth-curve.png){#fig-growth-curve fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

## Appendix figure set

### CPUE residuals

<!-- figure: cpue-residuals -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![CPUE residuals. Values show observed minus fitted CPUE for selected index fisheries; facets and colours, line styles, or legends identify assessment models and CPUE series.](generated/outputs/figures/cpue-residuals.png){#fig-cpue-residuals fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Total catch residuals

<!-- figure: total-catch-residuals -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Total catch residuals. The figure uses the labels, colours, facets, and legends shown in the plot to identify scenarios, fisheries, regions, years, or diagnostics selected in mfclshiny. It is generated from the Catch view.](generated/outputs/figures/total-catch-residuals.png){#fig-total-catch-residuals fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Catch residuals by fishery

<!-- figure: catch-by-fishery-residuals -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Catch residuals by fishery. The figure uses the labels, colours, facets, and legends shown in the plot to identify scenarios, fisheries, regions, years, or diagnostics selected in mfclshiny. It is generated from the Catch view.](generated/outputs/figures/catch-by-fishery-residuals.png){#fig-catch-by-fishery-residuals fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Length-frequency fits

<!-- figure: length-frequency -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Observed and fitted length-frequency samples. Bars show observed sample counts and fitted overlays show model predictions; panels identify selected fisheries and years, and colours, line styles, or legends identify assessment models.](generated/outputs/figures/length-frequency.png){#fig-length-frequency fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Length-frequency residuals

<!-- figure: length-frequency-residuals -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Length-frequency bubble residuals. Bubble size and sign show observed-minus-fitted residual structure by length bin, fishery, and year for the selected assessment models.](generated/outputs/figures/length-frequency-residuals.png){#fig-length-frequency-residuals fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Fishery selectivity at age

<!-- figure: fishery-process -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Age-based fishery selectivity. Curves show estimated selectivity by age class for selected fisheries; facets and legends identify fisheries and assessment models.](generated/outputs/figures/fishery-process.png){#fig-fishery-process fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Fishery selectivity at length

<!-- figure: fishery-selectivity-length -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Length-based fishery selectivity. Curves show estimated selectivity by length for selected fisheries; facets and legends identify fisheries and assessment models.](generated/outputs/figures/fishery-selectivity-length.png){#fig-fishery-selectivity-length fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage

### Fishery selectivity at weight

<!-- figure: fishery-selectivity-weight -->
\vspace*{0.04\textheight}

![Weight-based fishery selectivity. Curves show estimated selectivity by weight for selected fisheries; facets and legends identify fisheries and assessment models.](generated/outputs/figures/fishery-selectivity-weight.png){#fig-fishery-selectivity-weight fig-align="center" width=100%}

\FloatBarrier
\clearpage
